description: Oh, sir, I do apologize for stepping in instead of Edgelord — the Guidelight Optimizer / Guidelight Matrix pair could have taken a 1:1 mirror, certainly they could, but it would have been a thinner solution than the alternative. The Guidelight Voyagers are one of ten canonically-named Wizards racing teams in Aetherdrift, with a hive-mind captain (Mendicant Core), a documented Omenpath-displacement origin, and a third known team card (Guidelight Pathmaker) not yet in corpus that would have re-orphaned any 1:1 edge the moment it landed. The Endriders precedent already exists in the layer for exactly this faction-shape; mirroring it for the Guidelight Voyagers means future Aetherdrift Guidelight cards attach to the node cleanly instead of accumulating duplicate edges around an absent center. I hope you don't mind, sir.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Magnezone ME-047 <-> Klink BB-061 mechanical-creature mirror
other_candidates_considered.verdict: refused — shared tag bridge (steel/machine/construct/gear) is the hub layer's job, not an edge. No designer-coordinated cohort, no canonical narrative connection, just two unrelated mechanical Pokemon that happen to share metal-and-gear iconography. Would have been a fabricated edge.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Guidelight Optimizer <-> Guidelight Matrix 1:1 same-faction mirror
other_candidates_considered.verdict: subsumed by this node proposal — drawing that edge now and then revisiting it when Guidelight Pathmaker enters corpus would mean three duplicate edges around an absent center. Node is the right shape; edges to the node will draw themselves as cards arrive.

next_pass_suggestions: When Guidelight Pathmaker enters corpus: add characters: [guidelight-voyagers] to its frontmatter and append to the node's appears_on list.

When Mendicant Core gets a printed card (current status unknown): create sibling character node mendicant-core.md and edge it to guidelight-voyagers.md per the captain-of-faction precedent (Far Fortune, End Boss <-> endriders).

Triple-thesis hub-attribution review: the labor hub edge here is curatorial-overlay-tier (Potion rule sub-rule 1) — automaton-as-instrument-of-hive-mind is BBL's read on Wizards' stated 'lost travelers seeking home' framing. The node body names which is which. Future Edgelord audit may want to re-check that the related_hubs list still parses correctly after triple-thesis re-reads.
